title: The MACU Report — Episode 25 — "The Last Pitcher"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

title_assets.the_last_pitcher: episodes/ep-025/titles/the_last_pitcher.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes house-style THE LAST PITCHER card; flag for build)

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

sweet.core: a smooth calm wasteland dealer in a pristine pale apron over a collared shirt, holding a paper packet of powder like a sommelier, a faint knowing smile, behind a folding table stacked with paper packets

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, big grin

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed, gold chain, theatrical posing

daryl.core: a strung-out wasteland regular, gaunt, wide-eyed, twitchy, clutching a sweating paper cup

warner.core: a sober earnest gaunt man in a frayed coat holding up a paper packet, reading the label, urgent

broll.crystal_packets: a pyramid of plain paper drink-mix packets on a folding table at dusk
broll.lemonade_stand: a child's wooden lemonade stand re-purposed as a wasteland dealing corner, a hand-lettered CRYSTAL sign
broll.ron_mug: a television news anchor's coffee mug full of pale cloudy lemonade on a news desk, a torn paper packet beside it
broll.crystal_pitcher: a sweating glass pitcher of pale lemonade with a ladle, condensation, on a news desk
broll.cut_lab: a grimy back-room table where pale powder is cut with ash and sand, scales, a single hanging bulb
broll.glass_water: a plain glass of clear water on an empty news desk
broll.stride_watch: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background
broll.safe: a vintage wooden console cabinet with a single round glowing glass lens, a cozy 1950s living room, soft lamplight
broll.everwell: an upright pale-wood casket pod with a single round glowing dial in a softly lit funeral showroom
broll.vending_machine: a battered glass-front vending machine with a glowing dial face, an empty slot, in an empty concrete room

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. Ron here. One day clean. I had water this morning. Just water. It was boring. It was so boring. I'm told that's the point.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And I'm Walter. Ron's had water all day. He's described it as "boring" eleven times. We're keeping a tally.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Twelve.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Good to have you back, Ron. The news.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: To the markets, where crystal has collapsed entirely. Once the settlement learned there was nothing in it, demand evaporated. It turns out people will chase anything — except a thing they're told is fine. Tooth futures are up. Everyone's biting again.

cues.speaker: DARYL
cues.vo: So, like... it was lemonade. The whole time. I traded my shoes for lemonade. ...I mean, it was GOOD lemonade. I'm not gonna stand here and say it was bad lemonade. But yeah. I got my shoes back. We all just kinda... gave each other our shoes back. Took us a while to sort out whose was whose. A few guys just kept walking. We let 'em.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Crystal's DEAD, baby, I'm not gonna pretend! But Dr. Goldtooth don't MOURN, Dr. Goldtooth PIVOTS! Found a whole CRATE of this in the SAME warehouse — little brown crystals, you put 'em in HOT water, baby, and you do NOT sleep for TWO DAYS! They call it... "instant." I call it the FUTURE. First cup's on the house! Side effects are minimal, baby. Some shakin'. The hearing comes back. Mostly.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's coffee, Doctor. That's just coffee.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: ...Don't tell the settlement that, baby. Let 'em find OUT.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Dr. Goldtooth still holds a four-star rating. The fourth star is provisional, and increasingly nervous.

cues.speaker: SWEET
cues.vo: This is the last one, Ron. The very last packet from the original pallet. The one that started the week. I'm not selling it. I'm leaving it right here. First pour's on the house. But you already know about the second one.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...The second one's a conversation. You know what the worst part is, Sweet? It was never the lemonade. I'd have chased anything that made last week quieter for a minute. The packet just got there first.

cues.speaker: SWEET
cues.vo: It's sugar-free, Ron. That's the part that should scare you. Nothing in it but what you bring. Most men never figure out they brought it.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Then I'm bringing water. ...It's boring. ...Yeah. I'll take boring.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Ron. You missed every hydration reminder I sent this month. I just deleted them. Your heart rate hasn't been this calm since I started counting. Whatever you just poured out — that one's not mine to log.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Stop counting, would you? Some stuff you're not supposed to score.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: The STAND is CLOSED, brother. Ain't nobody to keep IN, ain't nobody to keep OUT. The last man who left this corner? ...He just LEFT. Walked on home. First time that ever HAPPENED on Roscoe's watch. ...Felt nice. OHHH yeah.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: ...Jesus.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: You know, MACU, they'll chase anything that promises to make the quiet go away. Turns out the quiet was never the thing to be scared of. Anyway. That's the news.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...That was almost wise, Walter.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: I have been told that, yes. I never know which ones.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And your forecast — a clear, settled weekend. Highs in the pleasant, a soft breeze, and for the first time all week, nobody standing on a corner waiting for anything. The settlement is just... outside. Drinking water. Looking up. Condition rating: extremely mild — and for once, that's a compliment.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. The crystal's gone, the stand's closed, and I'm still here. It wasn't a good week. But it ended better than it started, and lately I'll take that. Thank you for staying with me.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: And to anyone out there still waiting on a corner for something to make the quiet go away — it's sugar-free. There was never anything in the packet but what you brought to it. So skip the second pour, and pour yourself a glass of water. It's boring. Turns out that's the part that's supposed to save you.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Well said. Anyway. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And tune in next week for a new installment of the Mayor Awesome Cinematic Universe — whatever the settlement gets itself into next. Knowing us... something.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Boring. ...Good.
